plan.summary: Implement a dark mode feature for the website, allowing users to toggle between light and dark themes, with their preference saved locally for persistence across sessions.

steps.description: Define CSS variables for light and dark themes. This involves creating a set of custom properties (CSS variables) under the `:root` selector for the default (light) theme, and then overriding these variables within a specific class (e.g., `.dark-theme`) that will be applied to the `body` element when dark mode is active.
Update existing component styles to use the newly defined CSS variables. This ensures that all elements on the page will automatically adapt to the current theme.
steps.details: Go through your existing CSS rules and replace hardcoded color values (e.g., `#333`, `white`, `blue`) with their corresponding CSS variables (e.g., `var(--text-color)`, `var(--background-color)`, `var(--primary-color)`). This step might involve modifying multiple CSS files depending on your project structure.

steps.description: Add a theme toggle UI element (e.g., a button or switch) to the website's header or a prominent location, allowing users to manually switch between light and dark modes.

Implement JavaScript logic to handle theme switching. This includes toggling the `dark-theme` class on the `body` element, saving the user's preference to `localStorage`, and applying the saved theme on page load to ensure persistence.

Perform comprehensive testing across various browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and devices (desktop, tablet, mobile) to ensure the dark mode functionality and styling are consistent and free of visual bugs. Verify that the toggle works, preference persists, and all UI elements are correctly styled in both modes.
steps.details: Manually test the theme toggle. Clear `localStorage` and refresh to check system preference detection. Check all pages and components for unstyled elements or contrast issues. Pay attention to images, icons, and third-party widgets that might not automatically adapt.

steps.description: Conduct an accessibility review to ensure that the color contrast ratios for text and interactive elements meet WCAG (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines) standards in both light and dark modes.
steps.details: Use browser developer tools (e.g., Lighthouse audit, Accessibility tab) or online contrast checkers (e.g., WebAIM Contrast Checker) to verify that text and background colors, as well as interactive elements, have sufficient contrast (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).

steps.description: Deploy the updated HTML, CSS, and JavaScript files to the production server.
steps.details: Follow your standard deployment procedures (e.g., commit changes to version control, run build process if applicable, deploy to hosting environment).

risks: Inconsistent styling: Some elements or third-party components might not correctly adopt the dark mode theme, leading to visual inconsistencies.
Flickering on page load: If the JavaScript to apply the saved theme runs too late, there might be a brief 'flash' of the default theme before dark mode is applied.
Accessibility issues: Poor color contrast in either light or dark mode could make content difficult to read for users with visual impairments.
Performance impact: While generally minimal, extensive use of CSS variables and JavaScript for theme switching could slightly impact initial page load or rendering performance on very complex sites.
plan.rollback_strategy: Revert the changes made to `index.html`, `style.css`, and `script.js` to their previous versions. This will remove the dark mode functionality and restore the website to its original state.
plan.success_criteria: The user can successfully toggle between light and dark modes using the provided UI element. The chosen theme persists across page loads and browser sessions. All website elements are consistently and correctly styled in both light and dark modes without visual bugs. Color contrast ratios meet accessibility standards in both themes.
plan.original_request: Add dark mode to my website

user_request: Add dark mode to my website
status_notes: Feature implementation failed: 'FeatureImplementationManager' object has no attribute 'implementation_plans'
